component_identification.content: Base components: 1 board, 1 Ever Tree, 128 Critter/Construction cards, 30 berry tokens, 30 twig tokens, 25 resin tokens, 20 pebble tokens, 24 wooden workers (6 per player color), 20 point tokens (10/3/1), 4 basic event tiles, 16 special event cards, 27 forest cards, 30 destination/other markers and setup pieces, and rulebook.

core_game_loop.content: Players alternate turns taking one of three options: place a worker, play a card to their city, or prepare for next season. Worker spots grant resources/cards/points and may be exclusive or shared. Card plays build your city engine (max 15 cards). When you prepare for season, you retrieve workers, gain season bonuses, and continue while others may still be in earlier seasons.

detailed_rules.content: To play a card, pay listed resources unless using occupancy discount (play a Critter for free if its matching Construction is in your city). Cards may trigger immediately (green), on production (green at season prep), destination effects, governance, or endgame scoring (purple prosperity). Basic events require specific card pairs in city; special events from cards can award points/resources. Forest locations vary each game and can significantly alter tempo. The Meadow has 8 shared face-up cards; when a Meadow card is taken, refill from deck. Hand limit is 8 (unless modified). City size is capped at 15 cards, but some cards (e.g., Travelers/unique rules) can create exceptions as printed. Game end for each player occurs when they cannot/will not take further actions after Autumn; because seasons are asynchronous, others may keep playing.

scoring_and_endgame.content: When all players have passed after Autumn actions, score: printed points on city cards, point tokens, event points (basic/special), prosperity card bonuses, and select card effects that award endgame points. Highest total wins; tie-breaker is most leftover resources, then shared.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Prioritize early resource conversion cards so each worker placement compounds later. 2) Use the free Critter-with-Construction pairing aggressively—it saves turns and resources. 3) Don’t rush seasons blindly; move up when retrieving workers is worth more than one extra current-season action. 4) Watch city cap (15) and reserve slots for high-value late prosperity cards. 5) Draft from Meadow tactically—deny opponents key pairs when possible.
